spec_match.rationale: The demo successfully demonstrates how text-fit: scale-down and balance dynamically adjust font size based on container width.

interactive.rationale: The width slider provides a smooth, real-time interactive experience to observe the text scaling behavior.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent index blurb accurately describes the headline scaler concept of preventing overflow without media queries.

edge_cases.rationale: While it compares scale-down and balance with long/short text, it does not explore extreme narrow width constraints or minimum font size limits.

references.rationale: The page includes a direct link to the ChromeStatus entry in the see also section.

design_system.rationale: The demo adheres to the design system, using standard CSS variables for colors, fonts, and spacing.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate extreme constraint edge cases
openQuestions.detail: The demo currently limits the width slider to 180px. It would be useful to show what happens when the container is extremely narrow (e.g., under 100px) where text must shrink to tiny sizes, and whether the browser enforces a minimum font size that eventually causes overflow anyway.

summary: A well-implemented interactive demo that clearly showcases the primary use cases of CSS text-fit (scale-down and balance) using a responsive container slider. It adheres to the design system and includes necessary references, but could be improved by exploring extreme constraint edge cases.
